name: checklist
title: Memory Care Tour Checklist
body:
Print or save as PDF
Family tour checklist
Memory Care Tour Checklist
Whispering Willows Memory Care
Mount Vernon, WA · (360) 208-7555
Community visited: ______________________
Bring this page to every community you tour. Ask the same questions at each, jot a quick note, and compare them side by side. There are no wrong questions — a good community welcomes all of them.
The 12 questions to ask
1. What is your staff-to-resident ratio — day and overnight?
2. How are staff trained specifically in dementia care, and how often?
3. What is your staff turnover, and how long have caregivers been here?
4. How do you keep residents safe from wandering and falls?
5. How do you build a personalized care plan, and how often is it updated?
6. Can my parent stay as the disease progresses (age in place)?
7. What does a typical day look like?
8. How is dining handled for someone with dementia?
9. How do you coordinate medical care and medications?
10. How — and how often — will you communicate with our family?
11. How do you handle a difficult day without over-relying on medication?
12. What are all the costs, and what would trigger a price increase?
Trust what you observe — green flags vs. red flags
✓ Green flags ✗ Red flags
Residents look engaged, clean, and cared for Residents parked unattended for long stretches
Staff greet residents warmly, by name Staff who can't tell you much about residents
A calm, home-like atmosphere; pleasant smell Strong odors, tension, an institutional feel
Secured but not a lockdown; safe outdoor space Either unsafe, or so locked-down it feels like confinement
Transparent about pricing and what raises it Vague on staffing, training, or true cost
Welcomes your questions and a second visit Pressure to sign quickly
Before the tour — 10 early warning signs of dementia to discuss
If several of these are happening and don't resolve, talk to a doctor (some causes are treatable). Source: Alzheimer's Association.
Memory loss that disrupts daily life
Trouble planning or solving problems
Difficulty completing familiar tasks
Confusion with time or place
Trouble with visual images & spatial relationships
New problems with words
Misplacing things, can't retrace steps
Decreased or poor judgment
Withdrawal from work or social activities
Changes in mood and personality
✦ Two visits beat one
Whenever you can, visit twice — and make one stop unannounced or at mealtime. The truest picture of a community is the ordinary day you weren't expected to see.
Educational checklist — not a substitute for professional medical or financial advice. Every family's needs differ; tour and verify in person. © 2026 Whispering Willows Memory Care · (360) 208-7555 · 2311 E Division St, Mount Vernon, WA 98274
